# R1.02 · Write-through

> A consistency-first strategy: every write goes to both layers in one synchronous step, so a read immediately after
> a write is always fresh — at the cost of write latency. Grounded in `EchoStore.Table.put/3,4` (a synchronous
> `SET … PX` to L2 then an L1 insert, framed with the write's mint-time version); real Valkey SET PX / GET. Served at
> /redis-patterns/caching/write-through.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/caching/write-through): the pattern and its three dives.
- [The synchronous dual write](/redis-patterns/caching/write-through/dual-write): `handle_call({:put,…})` sets L2 with `SET … PX` then inserts L1, before the write returns.
- [The consistency guarantee](/redis-patterns/caching/write-through/consistency): why the next read cannot be stale — the `version<>value` frame and `Coherence.newer?` — against cache-aside.
- [The latency cost](/redis-patterns/caching/write-through/latency-cost): the synchronous `GenServer.call({:put,…})` pays the L2 round-trip on the caller's path; the failure modes when one layer fails.

## Up

- [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
